title: Publish order validation and automation script
purpose: Document the correct crates.io publish order, validate it with dry-runs, and provide an automation script so the team can publish reliably.
task: Derive the publish order from the dependency graph. Write a publish script (scripts/publish-crates.sh) that iterates crates in order, runs cargo publish --dry-run by default, and accepts a --live flag for actual publication. Validate the order with a full dry-run pass. Document the publish process in the repo.

requirements.title: Publish order derivation and documentation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL document the leaf-first publish order based on the workspace dependency graph. The order SHALL be: aion-core, aion-store, aion-store-libsql, aion-package, aion-nif, aion, aion-proto, aion-server, aion-worker, aion-client, aion-cli.
acceptance: Publish order is documented in scripts/publish-crates.sh and in the repo README.
The order matches the actual dependency graph (verified by cargo metadata).
No crate depends on a crate that appears later in the publish order.

requirements.title: Full dry-run validation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL pass cargo publish --dry-run for every crate when executed in the documented publish order.
acceptance: Running scripts/publish-crates.sh with no flags performs a dry-run of all crates.
All 11 crates pass dry-run without errors.
The script exits non-zero on the first failure.

requirements.title: Publish automation script
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide scripts/publish-crates.sh that iterates all workspace crates in dependency order and runs cargo publish for each. Default mode is --dry-run. A --live flag publishes for real.
acceptance: Default invocation runs cargo publish --dry-run for each crate.
scripts/publish-crates.sh --live runs cargo publish (no --dry-run) for each crate.
The script logs each crate name and result as it proceeds.
The script stops on the first failure.

boundaries: SHALL create a publish script that handles the full ordered publish.
SHALL validate the dependency graph matches the publish order.
SHALL NOT actually publish to crates.io — this brief validates with --dry-run only.
SHALL NOT set up CI/CD publishing (that is an operational concern beyond this brief).
SHALL NOT change any crate code or dependencies (PK-001 handles that).
verification: scripts/publish-crates.sh runs successfully in dry-run mode.
All 11 crates report success from cargo publish --dry-run.
shellcheck scripts/publish-crates.sh passes (if shellcheck is available).
